topic.what: A randomised controlled trial evaluating STW 5-II, a standardised licorice-containing herbal preparation (a simplified version of the STW 5 / Iberogast formula), for functional dyspepsia; STW 5-II produced significant symptom improvement compared with placebo.
topic.how: Double-blind RCT in patients with Rome II-defined functional dyspepsia; patients randomised to STW 5-II liquid extract or placebo for 4 weeks; primary endpoint was the Gastrointestinal Symptom Score (GIS); secondary endpoints included individual symptom VAS scores.
topic.why_matters: Functional dyspepsia is highly prevalent with limited effective pharmacotherapy; a well-designed RCT demonstrating that a multi-herb preparation containing licorice achieves significant symptom relief provides evidence-based support for phytotherapy in this indication.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction in validated composite dyspepsia symptom score after 4 weeks of treatment
characteristics.value: Significantly greater reduction in STW 5-II arm vs. placebo (p < 0.01)
characteristics.context: Functional dyspepsia (Rome II); mixed dysmotility and ulcer-like subtypes
Includes both ulcer-like and dysmotility-like dyspepsia subtypes

characteristics.what_it_measures: Incidence of adverse events, withdrawal rate
characteristics.value: Well tolerated; adverse event rate comparable to placebo
characteristics.context: Short-term (4-week) exposure; long-term glycyrrhizin effects not assessable

good_for: Patients with functional dyspepsia seeking evidence-based phytotherapy
Clinicians considering STW 5 or STW 5-II as first- or second-line dyspepsia management
Research into multi-herb GI preparations with licorice as an active component
not_for: H. pylori-positive organic peptic ulcer (licorice-based therapy not a substitute for eradication)
Long-term use without monitoring for glycyrrhizin-related adverse effects (hypertension, hypokalaemia)
Conditions requiring direct antimicrobial or acid-suppressive therapy
requires: Standardised extract with defined glycyrrhizin and flavonoid content
Distinction from DGL (deglycyrrhizinated licorice) — STW 5-II retains glycyrrhizin
Follow-up for >4 weeks to assess durability of symptom relief

novelty: Demonstrates in an RCT that a licorice-containing herbal combination (STW 5-II) produces clinically meaningful dyspepsia symptom relief, lending credibility to phytotherapy in a condition with unmet pharmacological need
Uses validated GIS outcome measure, improving comparability with pharmacological dyspepsia trials
Provides evidence for the simplified STW 5-II formulation specifically, allowing attribution to its constituent herbs including licorice root
open_problems: Isolating the contribution of licorice vs. other herbs in STW 5-II to the observed therapeutic effect
Optimal duration of therapy and risk of glycyrrhizin-related adverse effects with extended use
Mechanism of action: prokinetic, mucosal protective, anti-spasmodic, or combined
Efficacy in H. pylori-associated dyspepsia specifically vs. pure functional dyspepsia
